channel.title: 2026-06-18 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-06-18 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Quantifying Quantum Computational Advantage on a Processor of Ultracold Atoms
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. X
👤 Yong-Guang Zheng, Ying-Chao Shen, Wei-Yong Zhang, An Luo, Ying Liu, Ming-Gen He, Hao-Ran Zhang, Wan Lin, Han-Yi Wang, Zi-Hang Zhu, Pei-Yue Qiu, Tian-Yi Wang, Ming-Cheng Chen, Chao-Yang Lu, Supanut Thanasilp, Dimitris G. Angelakis, Zhen-Sheng Yuan, and Jian-Wei Pan
Author(s): Yong-Guang Zheng, Ying-Chao Shen, Wei-Yong Zhang, An Luo, Ying Liu, Ming-Gen He, Hao-Ran Zhang, Wan Lin, Han-Yi Wang, Zi-Hang Zhu, Pei-Yue Qiu, Tian-Yi Wang, Ming-Cheng Chen, Chao-Yang Lu, Supanut Thanasilp, Dimitris G. Angelakis, Zhen-Sheng Yuan, and Jian-Wei Pan An ultracold-atom processor demonstrates a utilizable quantum computational advantage by simulating the highly entangled dynamics of a driven many-body system. [Phys. Rev. X 16, 021057] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Nonstabilizerness and Error Resilience in Noisy Quantum Circuits
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Fabian Ballar Trigueros and José Antonio Marín Guzmán
Author(s): Fabian Ballar Trigueros and José Antonio Marín Guzmán We investigate how noise impacts nonstabilizerness—a key resource for quantum advantage—in many-body qubit systems. While noise typically degrades quantum resources, we show that amplitude damping, a nonunital channel, can generate or enhance nonstabilizerness, whereas depolarizing noise provably ca… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 240602] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Quantum Computational Complexity of Matrix Functions
item.description:
📚 PRX Quantum
👤 Santiago Cifuentes, Samson Wang, Thais L. Silva, Mario Berta, and Leandro Aolita
Author(s): Santiago Cifuentes, Samson Wang, Thais L. Silva, Mario Berta, and Leandro Aolita The complexity of different parameter regimes in matrix function problems is characterized, providing new insights into where quantum advantage may be possible. [PRX Quantum 7, 020364] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Equilibrium and dynamical quantum phase transitions in dipolar atomic Josephson junctions
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Cesare Vianello, Giovanni Mazzarella, and Luca Salasnich
Author(s): Cesare Vianello, Giovanni Mazzarella, and Luca Salasnich An atomic Josephson junction realized with dipolar bosons in a double-well potential can be described by an extended Bose-Hubbard model in which dipolar interactions generate an effective on-site interaction and nearest-neighbor pair tunneling. Using mean-field theory and exact diagonalization, we i… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 063318] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Anticoncentration and state design of doped real Clifford circuits and tensor networks
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Beatrice Magni, Markus Heinrich, Lorenzo Leone, and Xhek Turkeshi
Author(s): Beatrice Magni, Markus Heinrich, Lorenzo Leone, and Xhek Turkeshi We investigate the statistical properties of orthogonal, or real, Clifford circuits doped with magic and imaginary resources. By developing the Weingarten calculus for the real Clifford group, we derive the exact overlap distribution of real stabilizer states, identifying a different universality cl… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 062446] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Remote nonreciprocal optical-microwave entanglement and one-way steering via parametric amplification
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Kai-Wei Huang, Yi Ren, Hao Zhai, and Hao Xiong
Author(s): Kai-Wei Huang, Yi Ren, Hao Zhai, and Hao Xiong We propose a scheme to generate remote nonreciprocal entanglement and one-way steering between spatially separated optical and microwave modes via parametric amplification. Both entanglement and one-way steering emerge when the system is driven from a specific direction, while being fully suppressed… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 063730] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Experimental measurement and a physical interpretation of quantum shadow enumerators
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Daniel Miller, Kyano Levi, Lukas Postler, Alex Steiner, Lennart Bittel, Gregory A. L. White, Yifan Tang, Eric J. Kuehnke, Antonio A. Mele, Sumeet Khatri, Lorenzo Leone, Jose Carrasco, Christian D. Marciniak, Ivan Pogorelov, Milena Guevara-Bertsch, Robert Freund, Rainer Blatt, Philipp Schindler, Thomas Monz, Martin Ringbauer, and Jens Eisert
Author(s): Daniel Miller, Kyano Levi, Lukas Postler, Alex Steiner, Lennart Bittel, Gregory A. L. White, Yifan Tang, Eric J. Kuehnke, Antonio A. Mele, Sumeet Khatri, Lorenzo Leone, Jose Carrasco, Christian D. Marciniak, Ivan Pogorelov, Milena Guevara-Bertsch, Robert Freund, Rainer Blatt, Philipp Schindler, Thomas Monz, Martin Ringbauer, and Jens Eisert This article shows that Rains’ quantum shadow enumerators, a powerful tool in quantum error correction, admit a direct physical interpretation in terms of outcome statistics of two-copy Bell measurements. This connection enables efficient learning protocols for these quantities and is demonstrated experimentally on a trapped-ion quantum device. [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023318] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Krylov-space anatomy and spread complexity of a disordered quantum spin chain
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Bikram Pain, David E. Logan, and Sthitadhi Roy
Author(s): Bikram Pain, David E. Logan, and Sthitadhi Roy We investigate the anatomy and complexity of quantum states in Krylov space, in the ergodic and many-body localized (MBL) phases of a disordered, interacting spin chain. The Krylov basis generated by the Hamiltonian from an initial state provides a representation in which the spread of the time-evol… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 214205] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Exact multivalley envelope function theory of valley splitting in Si/SiGe nanostructures
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Lasse Ermoneit, Abel Thayil, Thomas Koprucki, and Markus Kantner
Author(s): Lasse Ermoneit, Abel Thayil, Thomas Koprucki, and Markus Kantner Valley splitting in strained Si/SiGe quantum wells is a central parameter for silicon spin qubits and is commonly described with envelope function and effective mass theories. These models provide a computationally efficient continuum description and agree well with atomistic approaches when the con… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 245306] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Non-Hermitian higher-order topological insulators enabled by altermagnet engineering
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Xiang Ji, Dengfeng Wang, Tong Zhou, and Xiaosen Yang
Author(s): Xiang Ji, Dengfeng Wang, Tong Zhou, and Xiaosen Yang We show that proximity to an altermagnet provides an efficient route to engineering non-Hermitian higher-order topological phases. The proximity-induced altermagnetic order gaps the edge states of a topological insulator, thereby driving a transition from a first-order to a second-order topological … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 245420] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Enhanced performance of sudden-quench quantum Otto cycles via multiparameter control
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 R. S. Watson and K. V. Kheruntsyan
Author(s): R. S. Watson and K. V. Kheruntsyan The authors present a theoretical framework to investigate quantum thermodynamic Otto cycles operating under the simultaneous sudden quench of multiple externally tunable control parameters. Applying this formalism to both a harmonically trapped one-dimensional Bose gas and the transverse-field Ising model, they demonstrate that multiparameter control yields a significant cooperative enhancement to net work and efficiency, dramatically outperforming the combined output of individual single-parameter cycles. [Phys. Rev. A 113, 063319] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Entropic limitations on fixed causal order
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Matheus Capela and Kaumudibikash Goswami
Author(s): Matheus Capela and Kaumudibikash Goswami Quantum processes can exhibit scenarios beyond a fixed order of events. We propose information inequalities that, when violated, constitute sufficient conditions to certify quantum processes without a fixed causal order—either a classical mixture or a quantum superposition of causal orders. The ineq… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 062217] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: High-contrast Bragg atom interferometer for testing continuous spontaneous localization
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Huaiyu Zhu, Ju Liu, Tao Zhang, Qin Luo, Zhongkun Hu, and Minkang Zhou
Author(s): Huaiyu Zhu, Ju Liu, Tao Zhang, Qin Luo, Zhongkun Hu, and Minkang Zhou The continuous spontaneous localization (CSL) model is one of the most promising approaches to address the wave-function collapse problem in the measurement process of standard quantum mechanics. In this work, the effect of the CSL model on a Bragg atom interferometer is investigated. A Bragg interf… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 063317] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Incoherent repumping scheme in the ⁸⁸Sr⁺ five-level manifold
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Valentin Martimort, Sacha Guesne, Derwell Drapier, Vincent Tugayé, Lilay Gros-Desormeaux, Valentin Cambier, Albane Douillet, Luca Guidoni, and Jean-Pierre Likforman
Author(s): Valentin Martimort, Sacha Guesne, Derwell Drapier, Vincent Tugayé, Lilay Gros-Desormeaux, Valentin Cambier, Albane Douillet, Luca Guidoni, and Jean-Pierre Likforman Laser-cooled trapped ions are at the heart of modern quantum technologies and their cooling dynamics often deviate from the simplified two-level atom model. Doppler cooling of the ⁸⁸Sr⁺ ion involves several electronic levels and repumping channels that strongly influence fluorescenc… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 063109] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: LiANd₂(BO₃)₃ (A=Ba,phantomůle0.16em0exSr): A frustrated spin-hexamer lattice with cluster-liquid-like state and enhanced magnetocaloric effect
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Malik Ashtar, Xinyang Liu, Zhaotong Zhuang, Junsen Xiang, Zhaoming Tian, and Peijie Sun
Author(s): Malik Ashtar, Xinyang Liu, Zhaotong Zhuang, Junsen Xiang, Zhaoming Tian, and Peijie Sun The exploration of model systems exhibiting geometrical spin frustration remains a key avenue for uncovering emergent quantum phenomena. In this work, we report the synthesis and characterization of a different type of frustrated quantum magnets, LiANd₂(BO₃)_{… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 224435] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Nonreciprocal universal photon blockade in an asymmetrical cavity
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Xiuwen Xia, Xinqin Zhang, Xiaobing Luo, Chaofei Liu, Haozhen Li, Jabir Hakami, Jingping Xu, and Yaping Yang
Author(s): Xiuwen Xia, Xinqin Zhang, Xiaobing Luo, Chaofei Liu, Haozhen Li, Jabir Hakami, Jingping Xu, and Yaping Yang Inspired by the distinctive properties of universal photon blockade (UPB), we propose a two-photon Jaynes-Cummings model in an asymmetric cavity to achieve quantum nonreciprocity. Through theoretical analysis, we establish direction-dependent optimal conditions for perfect UPB in weak-driving limita… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 063729] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Quantum Mechanics Based on Real Numbers: A Consistent Description
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Pedro Barrios Hita, Anton Trushechkin, Hermann Kampermann, Michael Epping, and Dagmar Bruß
Author(s): Pedro Barrios Hita, Anton Trushechkin, Hermann Kampermann, Michael Epping, and Dagmar Bruß By exploiting a physically motivated principle, rather than a mathematical postulate, researchers offer a new perspective on how a real-valued quantum theory can be constructed. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 240202] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Quantum Semiconductor Heterostructures for meV Axion Dark Matter Detection
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Jaanita Mehrani, Tao Xu, Andrey Baydin, Michael J. Manfra, Henry O. Everitt, Andrew J. Long, Kuver Sinha, Junichiro Kono, and Shengxi Huang
Author(s): Jaanita Mehrani, Tao Xu, Andrey Baydin, Michael J. Manfra, Henry O. Everitt, Andrew J. Long, Kuver Sinha, Junichiro Kono, and Shengxi Huang We propose a novel strategy and a new class of detectors for the direct detection of axion dark matter in the meV mass range, based on resonantly enhanced axion-photon conversion through the inverse Primakoff effect in engineered radiometers composed of quantum semiconductor heterostructures. Semico… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 241001] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Quantum State Preparation without Coherent Arithmetic
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Sam McArdle, András Gilyén, and Mario Berta
Author(s): Sam McArdle, András Gilyén, and Mario Berta We introduce a versatile method for preparing a quantum state whose amplitudes are given by some known function. Unlike existing approaches, our method does not require handcrafted reversible arithmetic circuits, or quantum table reads, to encode the function values. Instead, we use a template quant… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 240603] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Second-quantized approach to the study of Halperin state in fractional quantum Hall effect
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Li Chen and Zhiping Yao
Author(s): Li Chen and Zhiping Yao We give a recursion relation for the second-quantized fermionic (bosonic) Halperin state, which avoids exact diagonalization of its two-component first-quantized parent Hamiltonian. We validate this formula by proving that the second-quantized Halperin state, as recursively defined in this formula, … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235133] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Stark effect in molecular Rydberg states: Calculation of Rydberg-Stark manifolds of H₂ and D₂ including fine and hyperfine structures
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 I. Doran, L. Jeckel, M. Beyer, Ch. Jungen, and F. Merkt
Author(s): I. Doran, L. Jeckel, M. Beyer, Ch. Jungen, and F. Merkt We present a general theoretical treatment and calculations of the fine and hyperfine structures in the spectra of high-n molecular Rydberg states in static uniform electric fields. The treatment combines (i) multichannel quantum-defect theory and long-range polarization models to determine the fi… [Phys. Rev. A 113, 062812] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Superconducting Berry Curvature Dipole
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Oles Matsyshyn, Giovanni Vignale, and Justin C. W. Song
Author(s): Oles Matsyshyn, Giovanni Vignale, and Justin C. W. Song Superconductivity and Bloch band Berry curvature responses represent two distinct paradigms of quantum coherent phenomena. The former relies on the collective motion of Cooper pairs while the latter proceeds from the momentum-space winding of Bloch wave functions. Here, we reveal a superconducting B… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 246902] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026

item.title: Universal Two-Excitation Scattering in Two-Dimensional Subwavelength Atomic Arrays
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Yidan Wang (王艺丹), Oriol Rubies-Bigorda, Valentin Walther, and Susanne F. Yelin
Author(s): Yidan Wang (王艺丹), Oriol Rubies-Bigorda, Valentin Walther, and Susanne F. Yelin Subwavelength atomic arrays are a leading platform for engineering strong light-matter interactions, presenting exciting opportunities for quantum science. However, a full understanding of their multiexcitation dynamics remains a significant challenge. In this Letter, we uncover a remarkable univers… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 243603] Published Thu Jun 18, 2026
